sheet_name: แบบขยายระบบสุขาภิบาลห้องน้ำ
view_title: แปลนระบบสุขาภิบาลห้องน้ำ 1 ( ท่อประปาเดินเหนือฝ้าเพดาน )

elements.description: Ø2"FD floor drain near the top of the room, adjacent to a cleanout
FCO (floor cleanout) symbol near the top-left, close to the FD
second FCO symbol near the bottom-left of the room
Ø1 1/2"V vent pipe near the lavatory/basin fixture at top-left
Ø2"W waste pipe running from the fixture cluster out to the right edge of the plan
Ø4"SW pipe exiting the top-right of the room toward the main stack
Ø4"S soil pipe from the WC (circle fixture symbol) at bottom-right, routed toward the wall/stack
second Ø2"V vent pipe at bottom-right, dashed line with downward arrow

sibling_files: บ้าน_เล็ก_2ชั้น_01_หน้า52_wc2_plan.json
warnings: This is a small-scale (1:50) schematic underfloor/overhead-piping detail of ground-floor ห้องน้ำ 1, explicitly noted '( ท่อประปาเดินเหนือฝ้าเพดาน )' -- cold-water piping routed ABOVE the ceiling for this bathroom, unlike house #2's equivalent bathroom detail which routed underfloor. grid_ref_is_free_text_not_grid_notation is correctly true -- this is not on the building's structural plan grid.

This is one of two side-by-side bathroom-detail views on this sheet (SN-07); the other (ห้องน้ำ 2, upper floor) is captured in the sibling file. This sheet is dedicated entirely to these two bathroom plans -- unlike house #2, where an equivalent single bathroom inset was combined onto the same sheet as the main floor plan.

Exact fixture layout (WC/lavatory positions relative to walls) and precise pipe routing paths are only partially legible at this resolution -- positions and pipe-size callouts are best-effort, not measured coordinates; flagged for zoom-review.

Note this sheet does NOT show a cold-water (CW) supply pipe-size callout for ห้องน้ำ 1 (unlike ห้องน้ำ 2's sibling view, which does show Ø3/4"CW) -- this may be because the CW line is routed above the ceiling per the sheet's own note and simply isn't diagrammed on this drainage-focused underfloor/overhead sketch, not a missing data point.
